body:
The JS template's example-app workflow has the same direct generated-push pattern that failed in link-assistant/calculator issue 195.

Reproducible example:
1. A push to main starts a workflow that regenerates preview/screenshot artifacts.
2. While that job is running, another main-writing workflow commits first, such as an automated release/version bump.
3. The preview job commits generated files and runs plain `git push origin HEAD:main`.
4. Git rejects the push with `main -> main (fetch first)` / `failed to push some refs` because the remote branch advanced.

Concrete evidence from calculator:
- https://github.com/link-assistant/calculator/actions/runs/28194099705 failed in Update Screenshots with a non-fast-forward push rejection.
- The failure happened while the release pipeline from the same merge was also writing to main.

Template location:
- `.github/workflows/example-app.yml` commits preview images with `git push origin HEAD:main` and no rebase/retry path.

Suggested fix:
- Put jobs that write generated files to main behind a shared main-writer concurrency group.
- Replace plain pushes with a helper that does `git push origin HEAD:main`, and on rejection runs `git pull --rebase origin main` before retrying.
- Add a small local regression test using a bare Git remote and two clones to prove the helper handles a non-fast-forward race.

Workaround until fixed:
- Manually rerun the failed generated-artifact workflow after the release/version-bump workflow finishes, or rebase the generated commit locally and push it again.
title: Harden generated preview-image pushes against non-fast-forward races
